briefdescription.para: Given an instance of CAN frame (can_msg)

detaileddescription.para: When CAN_MSG_INIT is used to initialize the frame
Then the frame has all fields initialized to zero

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called with an invalid mode value
Then -1 is returned, ERRNUM_INVAL error code is set

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with data length greater than the maximum value

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called with any mode value
Then -1 is returned, ERRNUM_INVAL error code is set

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Remote Transmission Request bit set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in no bit-stuffing mode
Then 47 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in no bit-stuffing mode
Then 47 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with data length equal to the maximum value

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in no bit-stuffing mode
Then 111 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Remote Transmission Request bit set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in worst-case estimate mode
Then 55 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in worst-case estimate mode
Then 55 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with data length equal to the maximum value

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in worst-case estimate mode
Then 135 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with CAN Identifier set and Remote Transmission Request bit set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with CAN Identifier set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with CAN Identifier set and 8 bytes of data

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with CAN Identifier set and 7 bytes of data

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with CAN Identifier set and 3 bytes of data

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Remote Transmission Request and Identifier Extension bits set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in no bit-stuffing mode
Then 67 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Identifier Extension bit set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in no bit-stuffing mode
Then 67 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Identifier Extension bit set and data length equal to the maximum value

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in no bit-stuffing mode
Then 131 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Remote Transmission Request and Identifier Extension bits set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in worst-case estimate mode
Then 80 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Identifier Extension bit set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in worst-case estimate mode
Then 80 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Identifier Extension bit set and data length equal to the maximum value

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in worst-case estimate mode
Then 160 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Identifier Extension and Remote Transmission Request bits set and CAN Identifier set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Identifier Extension bit set and CAN Identifier set

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Identifier Extension bit set, CAN Identifier set and 8 bytes of data

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Identifier Extension bit set, CAN Identifier set and 2 bytes of data

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a zero-initialized CAN format frame with Identifier Extension bit set, CAN Identifier set and 6 bytes of data

detaileddescription.para: When can_msg_bits() is called in exact mode
Then the size of the frame in bits with bit stuffing is returned

briefdescription.para: Given N/A

detaileddescription.para: When can_crc() is called with only zeroes and a null pointer
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given N/A

detaileddescription.para: When can_crc() is called with an initial CRC value, a pointer to a memory area, any offset and 0 bits to hash
Then the initial CRC value is returned

briefdescription.para: Given N/A

detaileddescription.para: When can_crc() is called with an initial CRC value, a pointer to a memory area, a negative offset and non-zero number of bits to hash
Then updated CRC value is returned by computing a CRC-15-CAN checksum of requested bits, based on the initial CRC value

briefdescription.para: Given N/A

detaileddescription.para: When can_crc() is called with an initial CRC value, a pointer to a memory area, a positive offset and non-zero number of bits to hash
Then updated CRC value is returned by computing a CRC-15-CAN checksum of requested bits, based on the initial CRC value

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen Library Unit Test Suite.

detaileddescription.para:
2020-2021 N7 Space Sp. z o.o.
Unit Test Suite was developed under a programme of, and funded by, the European Space Agency.
